name: Icon
description: Icon renders a single glyph from Cartrack's FontAwesome set — a thin wrapper around FontAwesomeIcon. It is the only icon system fleet-web actually uses; there is no Material icon set anywhere in the app despite MUI powering most other components. All icons must be registered once in src/util-functions/font-initialization.ts (via FontAwesome's `library.add(...)`) before they can be referenced by name.
keyRule: Always render icons through Icon rather than a raw <svg>, emoji, or CSS ligature. Reference an already-registered icon name; if the icon you need isn't registered yet, add it to font-initialization.ts first — don't import a fa* module ad hoc in a feature component.

variants.name: Solid
variants.whenToUse: Default weight for most UI icons (fas prefix, Free tier). Most of the app's registered icons are this style.

variants.name: Regular
variants.whenToUse: Lighter-weight alternative used selectively (far prefix, Free tier) — e.g. faCheckSquare, faCommentAlt, faClock.

variants.name: Light
variants.whenToUse: Thinnest weight, used for a specific set of vehicle/fleet glyphs (fal prefix). Requires the Pro tier and the FontAwesome Pro licence Cartrack already holds — e.g. faSteeringWheel, faBusAlt, faTaxi, faUserHardHat.

anatomy.name: Glyph
anatomy.description: The single SVG path rendered by FontAwesomeIcon, resolved at runtime from the icon name against the app-wide registered library.

behaviors.name: Name resolution
behaviors.description: `icon` is looked up against whatever was passed to `library.add()` in font-initialization.ts. A name that was never registered there renders nothing at runtime — there's no build-time check today.

behaviors.name: Colour inheritance
behaviors.description: Without an explicit `color` prop, the glyph fills with currentColor — it inherits whatever text colour is already in scope, same as any inline glyph.

behaviors.name: Sizing
behaviors.description: Without an explicit `size` prop, the glyph sizes to 1em — it scales with the surrounding font-size rather than needing its own explicit size in most call sites.

specs.value: xs, sm, lg, 1x–10x (FontAwesome's own enum — see tokens.json semantic.icon.sizing)

props.name: icon
props.description: The registered icon name, e.g. "chevron-down", or a [prefix, name] tuple (e.g. ["fal", "steering-wheel"]) to pick a specific style tier when more than one is registered under the same name.

props.name: size
props.description: FontAwesome's built-in size enum. Most real usage omits this and relies on inherited font-size instead.

props.name: color
props.description: CSS colour override. Omit to inherit currentColor — reuse semantic.color.text.* or a categorical hue.<hue>.icon token rather than a raw hex value.

props.name: pulse
props.description: Stepped 8-frame rotation, an alternative loading affordance to spin.

props.name: fixedWidth
props.description: Forces a consistent glyph width so mixed icons in a vertical list line up — matches FontAwesome's own fixed-width utility.

props.name: rotation
props.description: Rotates the glyph, e.g. reusing one chevron asset for both an expand and collapse affordance.

props.name: title
props.description: Native SVG <title> text. Not a substitute for an aria-label on the interactive control the icon sits inside — see Accessibility.

doThis: Reference an icon name already registered in font-initialization.ts.
Let colour and size inherit from context unless a specific override is genuinely needed.
Pair every icon-only control with an accessible name (aria-label or a Tooltip), not just this component's `title` prop.
dontDoThis: Don't import a fa* icon module directly in a feature component — register it centrally first.
Don't assume a Pro-tier icon (fal prefix, or any icon only in pro-*-svg-icons) is available to consumers of this design system without their own FontAwesome Pro licence.
Don't hardcode a hex colour on an icon — reuse semantic.color.text.* or the categorical hue.<hue>.icon tokens.
accessibility: Icon-only buttons need an accessible name from the surrounding control (aria-label, or a Tooltip per the Tooltip component's own rule) — the icon itself carries no text.
Purely decorative icons (next to a text label that already says the same thing) should be aria-hidden so they aren't announced twice.
Don't rely on icon shape alone to convey a status — pair it with the categorical colour and, where possible, text.

related.name: Tooltip
related.why: Names an icon-only control that has no visible text

related.name: Badge
related.why: Anchors a count/dot indicator to an icon

related.name: Button
related.why: leadingIcon takes a registered FontAwesome name, not a Material ligature

related.name: TextField
related.why: leadingIcon/trailingIcon take a registered FontAwesome name, not a Material ligature
